topic: Win Rate Improvement — New Feature Research

findings.summary: Require daily/4H/1H alignment before entry. Reduces false signals and helps counter bullish bias by filtering long entries when higher timeframes are bearish.
findings.upgrades: Signal Engine — adds timeframe confluence check before the 9-dimension scoring runs
findings.what_changes: Adds a new timeframe_confluence() function that checks daily, 4-hour, and 1-hour alignment. Only passes candidates to signal scoring when all three agree on direction.
findings.why_it_matters: The bot currently scores signals on one timeframe. This is a major source of false signals and the bullish bias — noisy hourly data triggers longs even when the daily trend is bearish.

findings.summary: Cap Kelly output with ATR-based volatility ceiling. Shrinks positions in high-vol regimes automatically.
findings.upgrades: Risk Management — wraps existing Kelly criterion with a volatility cap
findings.what_changes: Adds a volatility_adjusted_size() wrapper around the Kelly output. When 20-day ATR spikes, positions shrink. When markets calm down, positions expand.
findings.why_it_matters: The current Kelly sizing doesn't account for regime changes. A 2% position in calm markets carries the same dollar risk as 2% during a VIX spike — but the actual risk is very different.

findings.summary: Structured sentiment from Yahoo RSS + Finviz via Claude. Add as 10th signal dimension. SAPPO research shows Sharpe improvement 1.55 to 1.90.
findings.upgrades: Signal Engine — adds a 10th scoring dimension for news sentiment
findings.what_changes: New sentiment_score() function that sends 2-3 headlines per ticker to Claude, gets a -1 to +1 score, and wires it as dimension #10 in the signal scoring system.
findings.why_it_matters: The bot currently has 9 signal dimensions but no structured sentiment input. News can move stocks 5-10% in minutes. This gives the bot a leading indicator instead of reacting after the move.

findings.summary: Rolling 6mo train / 1mo test windows. Gold standard for validating the 9-dimension scoring weights.
findings.upgrades: Backtester — adds walk-forward validation mode alongside existing backtest
findings.what_changes: New walk_forward_test() function that splits history into rolling train/test windows, optimises signal weights on train data, and validates on unseen test data.
findings.why_it_matters: The 9-dimension scoring system has many tunable weights. Without walk-forward testing, those weights may be curve-fit to past data that won't repeat. This is the #1 way algo traders lose their edge.

findings.summary: 20-day rolling correlation matrix. Reduce correlated positions to prevent hidden concentration risk.
findings.upgrades: Risk Management — adds Layer 6: correlation guard for open positions
findings.what_changes: New correlation_check() function that calculates rolling 20-day correlations between all open positions. If correlation > 0.7, the smaller position gets reduced by 50%.
findings.why_it_matters: If the bot holds 4 tech stocks that are all 0.85 correlated, it effectively has one massive position disguised as four. One bad day wipes out weeks of gains.

findings.summary: 3-state HMM on vol + RSI. Adapts strategy aggressiveness to bull/bear/choppy. Detects regime shifts 1-2 weeks before MAs.
findings.upgrades: ML Engine — adds market regime classification that adapts all signal weights
findings.what_changes: Trains a Hidden Markov Model on 20-day volatility + RSI to classify markets as bull, bear, or choppy. In bull mode: favour momentum. In bear mode: favour mean reversion. In choppy: reduce size 50%.
findings.why_it_matters: A strategy optimised for bull markets bleeds money in choppy or bear markets. Regime detection catches the shift before moving averages confirm it, giving the bot a 1-2 week head start.

findings.summary: Approximate institutional accumulation from volume on up/down closes. Leading indicator even with free data.
findings.upgrades: Signal Engine — adds order flow as a new indicator alongside existing 9 dimensions
findings.what_changes: New order_flow_imbalance() function that calculates buy/sell volume ratio from up-close vs down-close bars. Extreme imbalance + technical confirmation signals institutional accumulation.
findings.why_it_matters: Volume tells you how much traded, but not who's buying or selling. Even with free data, the up-close/down-close split approximates institutional order flow — a leading indicator.

findings.summary: Shuffle backtest trade sequences 1000x to find true drawdown distribution. Catches luck-dependent edges.
findings.upgrades: Backtester — adds Monte Carlo simulation to validate backtest results
findings.what_changes: After a backtest runs, shuffles the trade sequence 1000 times and calculates the 95th percentile max drawdown. If it exceeds the in-sample drawdown by >50%, the edge may be luck.
findings.why_it_matters: A great backtest doesn't mean a great strategy — the order of trades matters. Monte Carlo reveals if your results depend on a lucky sequence or hold up under any ordering.
